title: WP: США развернули в Ормузском проливе шесть торговых судов
description: Военно-морские силы США развернули шесть торговых судов в Ормузском проливе, вышедших из иранского порта. Об этом сообщила газета The Washington Post со ссылкой на американских военнослужащих.

title: Scoop: Vance heading to Iowa for midterm events
description: Vice President Vance will head to Iowa later this month to host a pair of events ahead of the fall midterms, Axios has learned.Why it matters: Vance's April 30 appearances will be aimed at helping Republicans in this fall's elections. They'll also be part of his first visit to an early primary state ahead of a widely expected bid for president in 2028.Zoom out: Vance will appear with GOP Rep. Zach Nunn, who's in a swing district in southwestern I

title: Exclusive: U.K.-Ukraine duo beats Americans at their own drone game
description: The U.S. launched a killer-drone competition in February. It was won, according to public leaderboards, by a small British company with frontline Ukrainian experience and a manufacturing footprint in Atlanta.Why it matters: Skycutter's stateside success, after flying well under the radar, is a reminder that:Flash does not necessarily signal substance;Innovation exists outside the big-money bubbles of Silicon Valley and Washington;And the Russia-U

title: Mobility's new Big Three: Tesla, Waymo and Uber
description: As the race toward autonomy unfolds, a new U.S. "Big Three" in mobility is emerging: Instead of GM, Ford and Chrysler, it's Waymo, Tesla and Uber. Why it matters: Automotive and tech giants, along with many well-funded startups, are vying for a slice of the self-driving future. The big picture: Autonomous driving technology is no longer science fiction. It's here.Robotaxis are operating in about a dozen cities — with tests underway in many more —

title: Americans grow a bit friendlier toward China
description: Data: Pew; Chart: Axios VisualsThe percentage of Americans holding favorable views of China rose for the third straight year, according to an annual Pew survey, and has nearly doubled since 2023.Meanwhile, the share of Americans who view China as an "enemy" has fallen significantly over that time.Why it matters: Distrust of China hardened into a bipartisan consensus beginning in Trump's first term. It's now softening somewhat during Trump 2.0.The

title: Scoop: House Democrats introduce 5 articles of impeachment targeting Pete Hegseth
description: House Democrats will introduce five articles of impeachment against Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday accusing him of abuse of power, war crimes and other serious wrongdoing.Why it matters: The measure has virtually no chance of passing this Congress, but it is the latest sign that Democrats have coalesced around Hegseth as their new top target in Trump's Cabinet.Democrats previously pushed to impeach former Homeland Security Secretary

Trump signs law expanding 2016 HEAR act, canceling sunset clause and barring artworks' current owners from using certain legal tactics, such as switching courts

title: Swedish Armed Forces Release Photo Showing JAS 39 Gripens Escorting Russian Kilo-Class Submarine
description: The Russian submarine shadowed by the Swedish Gripens was transiting the Kattegat, a strategic waterway linking the Baltic approaches with the North Sea system. Although the spotlight is currently on Iran, the Russian military activity in the Baltic region continues to draw scrutiny. The Swedish Armed Forces have just released some interesting images showing JAS []

title: The president who threatened to end a civilization is supposed to guarantee Ukraine’s survival
description: Trump's Iran war has diverted diplomats and drained interceptor stockpiles — just as his envoys were supposed to deliver security guarantees to Kyiv.

title: Israeli army issues new evacuation call to south Lebanon residents
description: Israel's military on Wednesday issued a fresh evacuation order for residents of south Lebanon as it pressed on with an offensive against Hezbollah militants. It is the first evacuation order by the military since direct talks were held on Tuesday in Washington between Lebanon and Israel's ambassadors to the United States. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

title: After three years of war, exiled Sudanese find their place in Kenya
description: This Wednesday marks three years since the start of the civil war in Sudan, a conflict that has forced 14 million people to flee their homes. Some 4 million of them have left their country altogether. While many have fled to neighbouring countries, some have chosen Kenya. The capital Nairobi has become a hub for welcoming not only Sudanese refugees, but also political and even armed groups linked to the conflict.

title: US urges IMF, World Bank to abandon climate finance goals
description: The climate-denialist Trump administration is pushing the World Bank and International Monetary Fund to stop focusing on clean energy development projects. We take a closer look. But first: Russia vows to make up for any shortfall in Chinese oil supplies due to the US-Israeli war on Iran, and crude prices fall on hopes of renewed peace talks.

title: Italian papers united as PM Meloni faces off with Trump
description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, April 15, 2026: First, the Italian front pages rally around Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni as she stands up to US President Donald Trump. Next, war robots in Ukraine have captured Russian positions for the first time in history. Meanwhile, the war in Sudan enters its fourth year. Elsewhere, the World Press Photo awards have been given out. Finally, an unusual campaign takes the UK by storm.

title: Toddler among 10 killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza
description: Gaza's civil defence agency said on Tuesday that a toddler was among 10 people killed in separate Israeli strikes in the northern part of the Palestinian territory. Violence continues despite a ceasefire in the Gaza war that came into effect on October 10, with both Israel and Hamas regularly accusing each other of violations.

title: Artist Alexander Calder's mobiles come to life in a Paris exhibition
description: Three pieces of wire and string, a clothespin, a cork – these humble materials gave rise to the famous sculptures of American artist Alexander Calder, now on majestic display at the Louis Vuitton Foundation in Paris. Some 300 works, including 139 sculptures and 33 paintings, will enliven the vast galleries until August 16, as well as, for the first time, the museum’s garden designed by the American-Canadian architect Frank Gehry, who passed away

title: War and Peace: Israel brings Lebanon to negotiating table as airstrikes rain down on Beirut
description: François Picard welcomes Farès Boueiz, former Lebanese Minister of Foreign Affairs. The Israeli-Lebanese talks in the US cannot be defined as a "breakthrough", explains the former Lebanese minister. He sees this as a technical prelude to meaningful negotiations. What we are witnessing is the exploratory stage of diplomacy. According to Boueiz, the fundamental issue lies in the misalignment of objectives. Lebanon approaches these discussions seeki

title: Spain launches programme to offer amnesty to 500,000 undocumented migrants
description: As countries on both sides of the Atlantic ramp up deportations of undocumented migrants, Spain’s left-wing government on Tuesday prepared to give legal status to hundreds of thousands of irregular workers. Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has championed the amnesty as a way to not only give informal workers legal protections, but to also bring more money into a social security system increasingly under stress by the country's ageing population.

title: Pope to urge peace in Cameroon's conflict zone
description: Pope Leo XIV is to arrive in Cameroon on Wednesday, the second stage of an African tour shadowed by insults from US President Donald Trump and suicide attacks in Algeria on his first day. The pontiff is to meet Cameroonian President Paul Biya at the start of his four-day visit to the mainly French-speaking country, before heading to a conflict zone where English-speaking separatists have been fighting the army for nearly a decade.

title: Paris Saint-Germain beats Liverpool to reach the Champions League semifinals
description: Paris Saint-Germain’s grip on the Champions League trophy remains strong. The defending champion advanced to the semifinals of European club soccer’s biggest competition by dumping out six-time winner Liverpool on Tuesday. A 2-0 win at Anfield sealed a 4-0 aggregate victory and moved PSG a step closer to becoming only the second club to retain the trophy in the modern era after all-time king of Europe Real Madrid.

title: Frenchman scoops up 1 million euro Picasso painting in Paris raffle
description: A ⁠Frenchman won ​a Picasso painting worth 1 million euros ($1.18 million) in a non-profit raffle on Tuesday. The ​winner, Ari Hodara, a 59-year-old software engineer from Paris, was selected at random at Christie's auction house in Paris from 120,000 tickets sold at 100 euros apiece. ​The raffle's proceeds ‌will fund Alzheimer's disease research.

title: Lebanese 'cautiously optimistic' as talks with Israel unfold in Washington
description: Israel and Lebanon agreed to direct negotiations following talks in Washington on Tuesday that US Secretary of State Marco Rubio had hailed as a "historic opportunity" for peace. Lebanon's ambassador to the United States Nada Hamadeh Moawad called for a ceasefire. FRANCE 24's Cyril Payen reports from Beirut, Lebanon.

title: France and UK to co-host Hormuz shipping security talks
description: France and Britain will co-host a video-conference Friday of countries ready to contribute to a "purely defensive mission" to secure the Strait of Hormuz, the French president's office said Tuesday. President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer are to co-chair the meeting to discuss a plan to "restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz when security conditions allow it", it said.

title: France’s Ekitike to miss World Cup after Liverpool Champions League injury
description: Liverpool striker Hugo Ekitike had to be stretchered off in the Champions League defeat by Paris Saint-Germain.

title: Iran citizen held in France over pro-Palestine comments returns home
description: Release of Mahdieh Esfandiari comes a week after Iran released two French citizens held on espionage charges.

title: Will Viktor Orban’s Legacy Live On in Brussels, Even Without Him?
description: Viktor Orban, Hungary’s newly ousted prime minister, helped to finance a Brussels think tank that pushes his populist vision. It will outlast him, at least for a while.

title: More than 200 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack return home
description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those repatriated.

title: Swedish, Polish firms invest in TNT plants to quench Europe’s ammo thirst

title: 'Today the relationship between France and the African continent changes,' historian says
description: French lawmakers on Monday passed a bill to simplify the return of artworks looted during the colonial era to their countries of origin. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, historian Marie Cécile Zinsou, resident of the Zinsou Fondation, a contemporary art museum, explains that the restitution of artefacts looted from former colonies is "a moral obligation" and fundamental to the restoring of national identities in African countries.

title: 'War is development in reverse,' UNDP chief tells FRANCE 24
description: Amid uncertainty over the war in Iran, the International Monetary Fund has downgraded its outlook for the global economy under three different scenarios depending on how long the conflict will last. The conference is being held as the brutal war between the army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) enters its fourth year.

description: The new Controlled Reception Pattern Antenna (CRPA) is part of the Digital GPS Anti-jam Receiver (DIGAR) system installed in the F-15E Strike Eagle following a contract to BAE Systems in 2022. title: New Images Provide Clearest View Yet of Damage to U.S.-made AN/FPS-132 Early Warning Radar in Qatar
description: The imagery offers a closer look at the damage to the giant phased-array AN/FPS-132 radar struck during Iran’s attack on Qatar last month. Newly published photos by Al Jazeera circulated online offer the clearest view yet t0 the damage sustained by the U.S.-made AN/FPS-132 Block 5 (SSPARS) ballistic missile early-warning radar in Qatar which was []

title: Calls for tougher US bunkers, hangars go back years, analysts say
description: The Iran war has reignited a longstanding conversation about the need for the U.S. to protect its aircraft with underground bunkers and hardened shelters.

title: Iran leaves door open for peace talks as hunt for missing US pilot continues
description: The prospect of a U.S. service member alive and on the run in Iran comes days after Trump threatened to bomb Iran “back to the Stone Ages.”

title: Turkey pushes for larger role in Europe’s defense as Trump questions NATO
description: Shutting out Ankara from EU defense initiatives would cause more damage than the U.S. pulling troops from the continent, warned Turkey's defense minister.

title: US blockade of Iran will be major military endeavor, experts say
description: The U.S. military has not offered basic details yet about the blockade, including how many U.S. warships will enforce it.

title: US-Iran peace talks end without deal as delegations leave Pakistan
description: Each side blamed the other for the failure of the 21-hour negotiations to end fighting that has killed thousands and roiled the global economy.

title: French investigators target Élysée palace in events company contracts probe
description: French investigators raided the Elysee palace on Tuesday in relation to an events company contracts probe. Investigators from the financial and anti-corruption departments of the Paris police were looking into why Shortcut Events had been chosen to host important state events for more than two decades.

title: Middle East war live: Rubio hosts Israeli, Lebanese envoys for first direct talks in decades
'Race to the border' as Islamic State and JNIM clash in Niger
description: For the first time, the Islamic State Al-Qaeda affiliate JNIM have clashed in West Africa’s Niger. While previous confrontations between the two jihadist groups had taken place in the tri-border area with Mali and Burkina Faso, they are now spreading toward a new border region between Niger, Nigeria, and Benin, in what France 24’s Wassim Nasr describes as a “race to the border” as the two groups vie for resources and recruits.

title: 'No time to waste': Magyar vows new era in Hungary after ousting Orban
description: Hungarian election winner Peter Magyar promised on Monday to usher in a "new era" in Hungarian politics, restoring democratic standards and unblocking European Union funds, following a landslide victoryover a MAGA- and Moscow-aligned incumbentthat will likely anchor Budapest in the EU mainstream.

title: McDonald's, Iran and the pope: Trump's bizarre press conference
description: US President Donald Trump received a DoorDash delivery from McDonald's to the Oval Office during a press conference where he spoke about Iran and the pope.

title: ‘We are a stable, predictable country’: Spanish PM Sanchez in Beijing
description: Spains Pedro Sánchez and Chinas Xi Jinping stressed respect for international law and a shared “sense of justice”.

title: Sudan’s war still misunderstood after three years
description: April 15, 2026, marks three years since the war in Sudan broke out.

title: Why are the US, Iran arguing over duration of uranium enrichment ban?
description: The US reportedly wants Iran to stop enriching for 20 years. Iran has agreed to five years. Heres why that matters.

title: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
description: The official tells the BBC that the armed group will not move forward with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

title: US blockade of Iran ports irresponsible and dangerous, China says
description: China, the biggest buyer of Iranian oil, says the US naval blockade undermines an "already fragile ceasefire".

title: Italy Suspends Defense Pact With Israel, Further Straining Ties
description: The move by Italy, once considered a strong ally of Israel, reflected growing anger over Israeli aggression in the Middle East. President Trump criticized Italy’s stance on the war.

title: Israelis Don’t Feel Much Like Victors in War With Iran
description: The regime in Iran has not changed and the nuclear and missile threats have not been eliminated, leaving many Israelis to wonder what this was all for.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel and Lebanon Are Set for Rare Direct Talks as Strikes Go On
description: Israel launched new attacks in southern Lebanon in its campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah. As Israeli and Lebanese officials prepared to meet in Washington, the fighting in Lebanon remained a sticking point in the fragile cease-fire with Iran.

description:
